categories.name: stress
categories.empathy: I understand that exams and studying can feel overwhelming. It's completely normal to feel stressed during this time.
categories.guidance: You could try breaking your study sessions into smaller parts and taking short breaks to relax. A brief [Breathing Space](/breathe) can help clear your mind.
categories.support: You're doing your best, and that matters.

categories.name: anxiety
categories.empathy: I hear you, and it sounds like things feel really intense right now. Feeling anxious is tough, but you are not alone.
categories.guidance: Let's try a simple [Grounding Movement](/breathe?tab=ground) or naming 5 things you can see around you to help center yourself.
categories.support: Take it one moment at a time. I'm here for you.

categories.name: sadness
categories.empathy: I'm so sorry you're feeling this way. It is completely okay to feel sad and to let yourself experience those emotions.
categories.guidance: Maybe write down what's on your mind in a private journal, or try taking a [Movement Break](/breathe?tab=move) to shift your energy.
categories.support: Remember to be gentle with yourself today.

categories.name: anger
categories.empathy: It is completely valid to feel frustrated or angry when things go wrong.
categories.guidance: Try taking a quick physical break to walk around, or write down everything you're angry about on a piece of paper, then delete or shred it.
categories.support: Take all the space you need. Your feelings are valid.
